pi_items.source: WHO recommendation: 'population divided by 1.8' is the standard quantification algorithm for ITN mass campaigns (https://www.cdc.gov/malaria/php/public-health-strategy/insecticide-treated-nets.html, https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-021-23707-7). The ratio accounts for shared nets between household members.

Task design: The keyword 'ITN' is used in export_result.sh (line 30) to filter indicators by name pattern for verification. Verified through script analysis.
Task design: The keyword 'ITN' is used in export_result.sh (line 33) to filter visualizations by name pattern. Verified through script analysis.

verifier.py does not validate target_period='2022'. The verifier checks: constant value (1.8), indicator existence, denominator uses constant, numerator uses Population, and visualization uses indicator. Period is not validated.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Sierra Leone DHIS2 demo database (real, non-synthetic data from databases.dhis2.org). The constant value of 1.8 is verified as the WHO standard for ITN mass campaign planning (population per net ratio). The task requires creating a constant with this value, an indicator that divides Population by the constant, and a visualization showing the results. The specific period '2022' is mentioned in the task description but is NOT actually verified by the automated verifier, which only checks the existence and formula relationships of the metadata objects.
